mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.2 percentage point threshold over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer spending power, creating a compounding demand shock that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns specifically during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent indicates a sharp contraction in residential construction activity, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns due to anticipated downstream economic weakness.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of instances following a >5% housing starts decline.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and decelerating real GDP growth confirms a broad economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as equity valuations adjust to lower earnings.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to outperform a baseline market-neutral strategy with Sharpe ratio > 0.5 over 3-year out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding 0.3 percent trigger expectations of imminent monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show positive mean or insignificant predictive accuracy (p > 0.05) during high-inflation months.
